first_paragraph: **The Most Eminent Order of Islam Brunei**[[1]](#cite_note-1) ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Darjah Seri Ugama Islam Negara Brunei Yang Amat Bersinar*), also translated as **The Most Illustrious Islamic Religious Order of the State of Brunei**,[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) is an [order](/wiki/Order_(decoration) "Order (decoration)") of [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"). It was established on 1 August 1968 by [Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Sultan_Hassanal_Bolkiah "Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah") for service in the propagation, preservation and strengthening of the Islamic religion.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Order of Brunei

| --- | --- |

| Sovereign | [Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Hassanal_Bolkiah "Hassanal Bolkiah") |

| Next (lower) | [Order of Paduka Laila Jasa Keberanian Gemilang](/wiki/Order_of_Paduka_Laila_Jasa_Keberanian_Gemilang "Order of Paduka Laila Jasa Keberanian Gemilang") |

**The Most Eminent Order of Islam Brunei**[[1]](#cite_note-1) ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Darjah Seri Ugama Islam Negara Brunei Yang Amat Bersinar*), also translated as **The Most Illustrious Islamic Religious Order of the State of Brunei**,[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) is an [order](/wiki/Order_(decoration) "Order (decoration)") of [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"). It was established on 1 August 1968 by [Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Sultan_Hassanal_Bolkiah "Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah") for service in the propagation, preservation and strengthening of the Islamic religion.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

## Current classes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Islam_Brunei&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Current classes")]

The five classes of appointment to the Order are, from highest grade to lowest grade:[[3]](#cite_note-3)

| Class | [Post-nominal](/wiki/Post-nominal_letters "Post-nominal letters") | [Title](/wiki/Malay_styles_and_titles "Malay styles and titles") | [Ribbon bar](/wiki/Ribbon_bar "Ribbon bar") |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| First Class | PSSUB | Dato Paduka Seri Setia |  |
| Second Class | DSSUB | Dato Seri Setia |  |
| Third Class | SSUB | — |  |
| Fourth Class | SUB |  |
| Fifth Class | PUB |  |

## Recipients

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Islam_Brunei&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Recipients")]

### First Class

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Islam_Brunei&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: First Class")]

* Unknown – [Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Omar Ali Saifuddien III") – [Sultan of Brunei](/wiki/List_of_sultans_of_Brunei "List of sultans of Brunei")[[4]](#cite_note-4)
* Unknown – [Ismail Omar Abdul Aziz](/wiki/Ismail_Omar_Abdul_Aziz "Ismail Omar Abdul Aziz") – [State Mufti](/wiki/State_Mufti_of_Brunei "State Mufti of Brunei")[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* Unknown – [Yahya Ibrahim](/wiki/Yahya_Ibrahim "Yahya Ibrahim") – [Deputy Minister of Religious Affairs](/wiki/Ministry_of_Religious_Affairs_(Brunei) "Ministry of Religious Affairs (Brunei)")[[6]](#cite_note-6)
* Unknown – [Yusoff Ismail](/wiki/Yusoff_Ismail "Yusoff Ismail") – [Deputy Minister of Education](/wiki/Ministry_of_Education_(Brunei) "Ministry of Education (Brunei)")[[7]](#cite_note-:6-7)
* Unknown – [Mahmud Saedon](/wiki/Mahmud_Saedon "Mahmud Saedon") – Muslim scholar*[[8]](#cite_note-:22-8)*
* 1972 – [Zain Serudin](/wiki/Zain_Serudin "Zain Serudin") – Minister of Religious Affairs[[9]](#cite_note-:02-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* 1976 – [Abdul Hamid Bakal](/wiki/Abdul_Hamid_Bakal "Abdul Hamid Bakal") – Member of the [Legislative Council](/wiki/Legislative_Council_of_Brunei "Legislative Council of Brunei")[[11]](#cite_note-:2-11)
* 1976 – Metali Matyassin – [Manteri Ugama](/wiki/Manteri "Manteri")[[11]](#cite_note-:2-11)
* 1984 – [Abdul Aziz Juned](/wiki/Abdul_Aziz_Juned "Abdul Aziz Juned") – State Mufti[[12]](#cite_note-12)
* 1998 – [Badaruddin Othman](/wiki/Badaruddin_Othman "Badaruddin Othman") – Minister of Religious Affairs[[13]](#cite_note-:03-13)

### Second Class

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Islam_Brunei&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Second Class")]

* 1972 – [Abdul Rahman Taha](/wiki/Abdul_Rahman_Taha "Abdul Rahman Taha") – Member of Privy Council[[14]](#cite_note-:333-14)
* 1990 – Abang Razali Abang Zainuddin[[15]](#cite_note-:04-15)
* 1990 – Mohd Tamin Abdul Hamid[[15]](#cite_note-:04-15)
* 2002 – Adam Ahmad – Director of Pusat Da'wah Islamiah[[16]](#cite_note-:9-16)
* 2002 – Rajid Abdul Kadir – Director of Mosque Affairs[[16]](#cite_note-:9-16)
* 2003 – [Pengiran Mohammad Abdul Rahman](/wiki/Pengiran_Mohammad_Abdul_Rahman "Pengiran Mohammad Abdul Rahman") – Minister of Religious Affairs[[17]](#cite_note-17)
* 2013 – Abdul Aziz Yusof – Permanent Secretary at the Ministry of Religious Affairs[[18]](#cite_note-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19)
* 2017 – [Pengiran Mohammad Tashim](/wiki/Pengiran_Mohammad_Tashim "Pengiran Mohammad Tashim") – Judge of the Syariah High Court[[20]](#cite_note-:11-20)
* 2017 – Mazanan Yusof – Head of Research at the State Mufti Office[[20]](#cite_note-:11-20)
* 2022 – Dennie Abdullah – Assistant Mufti Ifta[[21]](#cite_note-:122-21)
* 2024 – [Norarfan Zainal](/wiki/Norarfan_Zainal "Norarfan Zainal") – Rector of [Universiti Islam Sultan Sharif Ali](/wiki/Universiti_Islam_Sultan_Sharif_Ali "Universiti Islam Sultan Sharif Ali")[[22]](#cite_note-22)

### Third Class

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Islam_Brunei&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Third Class")]

* Unknown – [Zasia Sirin](/wiki/Zasia_binti_Sirin "Zasia binti Sirin") – Member of the Legislative Council[[23]](#cite_note-23)
* 2010 – [Pengiran Bahrom](/wiki/Pengiran_Bahrom "Pengiran Bahrom") – Deputy Minister of Religious Affairs[[24]](#cite_note-24)
* 2011 – Mail Besar – Speaker from the Islamic Da'wah Centre[[25]](#cite_note-:1-25)
* 2011 – Mahani Musa – Acting Deputy Secretary of Brunei Islamic Religious Council[[25]](#cite_note-:1-25)
* 2011 – Rusma Kepli – Head of the Policy and Meeting Section of the Policy, Planning and Research Division[[25]](#cite_note-:1-25)
* 2017 – Siti Noor Dzuhairah – Champion of the National Musabaqah Al-Quran[[20]](#cite_note-:11-20)
* 2017 – Rosle Jumat – Acting Director of Administration at the State Mufti Office[[20]](#cite_note-:11-20)
* 2017 – Mas Raduan Jumat – Head of Buhuth at the [State Mufti](/wiki/State_Mufti_of_Brunei "State Mufti of Brunei") Office[[20]](#cite_note-:11-20)
* 2017 – Siti Noor Dzuhairah – [Qariah](/wiki/Al-Qaria "Al-Qaria") runners-up at the 59th International Level Al-Quran Recitation Ceremony in Kuala Lumpur[[20]](#cite_note-:11-20)
* 2022 – Muhammad Bahrin Alias – Chief Registrar of the Syariah Appeal Court[[21]](#cite_note-:122-21)
